sections.paragraph:
φρήν, φρενός, ἡ pl. αἱ φρένες (mostly pl. and in various senses: Hom. et al.; ins, pap, LXX, TestSol, Philo) in our lit. only in one place and only in the pl. as the process of careful consideration, thinking, understanding (Hom. et al.; Plut., Mor. 116b φρένας ἔχειν; Herm. Wr. 13, 4; 5; Pr 7:7; 9:4 al.; Jos., Ant. 10, 114 ἀπολέσαντες τὰς φρένας Hippol., Ref. 4, 28, 6) 1 Cor 14:20ab.—B. 1198. SSullivan, A Study of φρένες in Pindar and Bacchylides: Glotta 67, ’89, 148–89, An Analysis of φρένες in the Gk. Lyric Poets (Excluding Pindar and Bacchylides): Glotta 66, ’88, 26–62; s. also lit. cited by GMachemer, HSCP 95, ’93, 121 n. 13.—DELG. M-M. TW. Sv.

  1. pl. = plural

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Hippol Hippol , II–III a.d.—List 5

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

Bacchylides Bacchylides , V b.c.—List 5

Gk. Gk. = Greek

HSCP HSCP = Harvard Studies in Classical Philology—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1065
